title: Data and code for: A comparison of generalised urban block 3D model variants considering attributes with an influence on urban analyses
author: Domínguez-Lapeña, Alejandro
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
This repository contains the data and scripts used in the paper: 
 “A comparison of generalisation strategies for 3D urban block models and their impact on urban analyses” (AGILE 2026). 
 The dataset is based on Spanish cadastral vector data for an urban block in Zaragoza and includes derived attributes such as building-part height (BP_HEIGHT) and auxiliary volume (AUX_VOL). 
 The repository implements a reproducible workflow for generating and analysing multiple 3D urban block model variants through three main generalisation dimensions: 
 1. Footprint simplification (horizontal aggregation) 2. Height simplification (vertical discretisation) 3. Envelope extraction (semantic filtering of surfaces) 
 These steps are combined to generate alternative 3D representations, which are evaluated in terms of volume, surface area, and object count, as indicators relevant for urban analyses. 
 The workflow integrates QGIS (Python console), GeoPandas/NumPy (Python), and Blender (bmesh), and is documented through structured scripts and example execution guidelines. 
 The repository is intended to support reproducibility of the study and to illustrate how different generalisation strategies influence geometry-sensitive urban analyses. 
 Additional outputs and example results may be added in future versions.
